title: Skin Lesion Classification Using Deep Learning: A CNN-Based Approach
author: Tiju Alex
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0

steps.instruction: Abstract— This study delves into the pressing issue of skin cancer, a dangerous condition caused by damaged DNA triggering uncontrollable cell growth. Traditional diagnosis methods face challenges like light reflections, varied color illuminations, and diverse lesion shapes. To address this, we propose a Deep Convolutional Neural Network (DCNN) model. Our approach involves preprocessing steps, including noise removal and feature extraction, coupled with data augmentation techniques. Comparative evaluations against established transfer learning models validate the robustness of our DCNN model on the HAM10000 dataset. By sidestepping accuracy details, our research signifies a significant leap forward in skin cancer detection, promising improved diagnostic accuracy and efficiency in the face of this deadly disease.
